sections.label: Section 853
sections.original: καὶ σοὶ τόδʼ ἐστὶν ἐξ ἐμοῦ · πόσιν δὲ σὸν
sections.translation: It is farewell I bid you too , lady ; and I go within the tent to seek your husband .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: ὦ ξένʼ , Αἰακοῦ γένεθλον , μεῖνον · ὦ , σέ τοι λέγω ,
sections.translation: Stranger of the race of Aeacus , stay awhile ! Ho there ! I mean you , O goddess-born , and you , daughter of Leda .

sections.label: Section 857
sections.original: τίς ὁ καλῶν πύλας παροίξας ; ὡς τεταρβηκὼς καλεῖ .
sections.translation: Who is it calling through the half-opened door ? What fear his voice betrays !

sections.label: Section 858
sections.original: δοῦλος , οὐχ ἁβρύνομαι τῷδʼ · ἡ τύχη γὰρ οὐκ ἐᾷ .
sections.translation: A slave ; of that I am not proud , for fortune does not permit it .

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: τίνος ; ἐμὸς μὲν οὐχί · χωρὶς τἀμὰ καὶ Ἀγαμέμνονος .
sections.translation: Whose slave are you ? not mine ; for mine and Agamemnon ’ s goods are separate .

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: τῆσδε τῆς πάροιθεν οἴκων , Τυνδάρεω δόντος πατρός .
sections.translation: I belong to this lady who stands before the tent , a gift to her from Tyndareus her father .

sections.label: Section 861
sections.original: ἕσταμεν · φράζʼ , εἴ τι χρῄζεις , ὧν μʼ ἐπέσχες οὕνεκα .
sections.translation: I am waiting ; tell me , if you are desirous , why you have stopped me .

sections.label: Section 862
sections.original: ἦ μόνω παρόντε δῆτα ταῖσδʼ ἐφέστατον πύλαις ;
sections.translation: Are you really all alone here at the door ?

sections.label: Section 863
sections.original: ὡς μόνοιν λέγοις ἄν , ἔξω δʼ ἐλθὲ βασιλείων δόμων .
sections.translation: To us alone will you address yourself ; come forth from the king ’ s tent .

sections.label: Section 864
sections.original: ὦ Τύχη πρόνοιά θʼ ἡμή , σῴσαθʼ οὓς ἐγὼ θέλω .
sections.translation: O Fortune and my own foresight , preserve whom I desire !

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ὁ λόγος ἐς μέλλοντʼ ἂν ὤση χρόνον · ἔχει δʼ ὄγκον τινά .
sections.translation: That speech will save [*] them in the future ; it has a certain pompous air .

sections.label: Section 866
sections.original: δεξιᾶς ἕκατι μὴ μέλλʼ , εἴ τί μοι χρῄζεις λέγειν .
sections.translation: Delay not for the sake of touching my right hand , if there is anything that you would say to me .

sections.label: Section 867
sections.original: οἶσθα δῆτʼ ἔμʼ , ὅστις ὢν σοὶ καὶ τέκνοις εὔνους ἔφυν ;
sections.translation: Well , you know my character and my devotion to you and your children .

sections.label: Section 868
sections.original: οἶδά σʼ ὄντʼ ἐγὼ παλαιὸν δωμάτων ἐμῶν λάτριν .
sections.translation: I know you have grown old in the service of my house .

sections.label: Section 869
sections.original: χὥτι μʼ ἐν ταῖς σαῖσι φερναῖς ἔλαβεν Ἀγαμέμνων ἄναξ ;
sections.translation: Likewise you know it was in your dowry king Agamemnon received me .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: ἦλθες εἰς Ἄργος μεθʼ ἡμῶν καὶ ἐμὸς ἦσθʼ ἀεί ποτε .
sections.translation: Yes , you came to Argos with me , and have been mine this long time past .

sections.label: Section 871
sections.original: ὧδʼ ἔχει . καὶ σοὶ μὲν εὔνους εἰμί , σῷ δʼ ἧσσον πόσει .
sections.translation: True ; and I bear all goodwill to you , less to your husband .

sections.label: Section 872
sections.original: ἐκκάλυπτε νῦν ποθʼ ἡμῖν οὕστινας λέγεις λόγους .
sections.translation: Come , come , unfold whatever you have to say .

sections.label: Section 873
sections.original: παῖδα σὴν πατὴρ ὁ φύσας αὐτόχειρ μέλλει κτενεῖν .
sections.translation: Her father , he that begot her , is on the point of slaying your daughter with his own hand .

sections.label: Section 874
sections.original: πῶς ; ἀπέπτυσʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , μῦθον · οὐ γὰρ εὖ φρονεῖς .
sections.translation: How ? That for your story , old man ! you are mad .

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: φασγάνῳ λευκὴν φονεύων τῆς ταλαιπώρου δέρην .
sections.translation: Severing with a sword the hapless girl ’ s white throat .

sections.label: Section 876
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγώ . μεμηνὼς ἆρα τυγχάνει πόσις ;
sections.translation: Ah , alas for me ! Does my husband happen to have gone mad ?

sections.label: Section 877
sections.original: ἀρτίφρων , πλὴν ἐς σὲ καὶ σὴν παῖδα · τοῦτο δʼ οὐ φρονεῖ .
sections.translation: No ; he is sane , except where you and your daughter are concerned ; there he is mad .

sections.label: Section 878
sections.original: ἐκ τίνος λόγου ; τίς αὐτὸν οὑπάγων ἀλαστόρων ;
sections.translation: What is his reason ? what vengeful fiend impels him ?

sections.label: Section 879
sections.original: θέσφαθʼ , ὥς γέ φησι Κάλχας , ἵνα πορεύηται στρατός .
sections.translation: Oracles , at least so Calchas says , in order that the army may start —

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: ποῖ ; τάλαινʼ ἐγώ , τάλαινα δʼ ἣν πατὴρ μέλλει κτενεῖν .
sections.translation: Where ? Alas for me , and for the one her father is going to kill !

sections.label: Section 881
sections.original: Δαρδάνου πρὸς δώμαθʼ , Ἑλένην Μενέλεως ὅπως λάβῃ .
sections.translation: To the halls of Dardanus , that Menelaus may recover Helen .

sections.label: Section 882
sections.original: εἰς ἄρʼ Ἰφιγένειαν Ἑλένης νόστος ἦν πεπρωμένος ;
sections.translation: So Helen ’ s return then was fated to affect Iphigenia ?

sections.label: Section 883
sections.original: πάντʼ ἔχεις · Ἀρτέμιδι θύσειν παῖδα σὴν μέλλει πατήρ .
sections.translation: You know all ; her father is about to offer your child to Artemis .

sections.label: Section 884
sections.original: ὁ δὲ γάμος τίνʼ εἶχε πρόφασιν , ᾧ μʼ ἐκόμισεν ἐκ δόμων ;
sections.translation: But that marriage — what pretext had it for bringing me from home ?

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: ἵνʼ ἀγάγοις χαίρουσʼ Ἀχιλλεῖ παῖδα νυμφεύσουσα σήν .
sections.translation: An inducement to you to bring your daughter cheerfully , to wed her to Achilles .

sections.label: Section 886
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ἥκεις ἐπʼ ὀλέθρῳ καὶ σὺ καὶ μήτηρ σέθεν .
sections.translation: On a deadly errand have you come , my daughter , both you , and I , your mother .

sections.label: Section 887
sections.original: οἰκτρὰ πάσχετον δύʼ οὖσαι · δεινὰ δʼ Ἀγαμέμνων ἔτλη .
sections.translation: Piteous the lot of both of you , and dreadful Agamemnon ’ s venture .

sections.label: Section 888
sections.original: οἴχομαι τάλαινα , δακρύων τʼ ὄμματʼ οὐκέτι στέγω .
sections.translation: Alas , I am undone ; my eyes can no longer hold their tears .

sections.label: Section 889
sections.original: εἴπερ ἀλγεινὸν τὸ τέκνων στερόμενον , δακρυρρόει .
sections.translation: If the loss [*] of children is painful , shed your tears .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: σὺ δὲ τάδʼ , ὦ γέρον , πόθεν φῂς εἰδέναι πεπυσμένος ;
sections.translation: From where , old man , do you say you had this news ?

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: δέλτον ᾠχόμην φέρων σοι πρὸς τὰ πρὶν γεγραμμένα .
sections.translation: I had started to carry you a letter referring to the former writing .

sections.label: Section 892
sections.original: οὐκ ἐῶν ἢ ξυγκελεύων παῖδʼ ἄγειν θανουμένην ;
sections.translation: Forbidding or combining to urge my bringing the child to her death ?

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: μὴ μὲν οὖν ἄγειν · φρονῶν γὰρ ἔτυχε σὸς πόσις τότʼ εὖ .
sections.translation: No , forbidding it , for your lord was then in his sober senses .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: κᾆτα πῶς φέρων γε δέλτον οὐκ ἐμοὶ δίδως λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: How comes it then , if you were really bringing me a letter , that you do not now deliver it into my hands ?

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: Μενέλεως ἀφείλεθʼ ἡμᾶς , ὃς κακῶν τῶνδʼ αἴτιος .
sections.translation: Menelaus snatched it from me , he who caused this trouble .

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον Νηρῇδος , ὦ παῖ Πηλέως , κλύεις τάδε ;
sections.translation: Do you hear that , son of Peleus , the Nereid ’ s child ?

sections.label: Section 897
sections.original: ἔκλυον οὖσαν ἀθλίαν σε , τὸ δʼ ἐμὸν οὐ φαύλως φέρω .
sections.translation: I have been listening to the tale of your sufferings , and I do not bear my own lightly .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: παῖδά μου κατακτενοῦσι σοῖς δολώσαντες γάμοις .
sections.translation: They will slay my child ; they have tricked her with your marriage .

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: μέμφομαι κἀγὼ πόσει σῷ , κοὐχ ἁπλῶς οὕτω φέρω .
sections.translation: Like you I blame your lord , nor do I view it with mere indifference .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: οὐκ ἐπαιδεσθήσομαί γε προσπεσεῖν τὸ σὸν γόνυ ,
sections.translation: No longer will I let shame [*] prevent my kneeling to you , a mortal to one goddess-born ; why do I affect reserve ? whose interests should I consult before my child ’ s ? Throwing herself before Achilles . Oh ! help me , goddess-born , in my sore distress , and her that was called your bride , in vain , it is true , yet called she was .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: σοὶ καταστέψασʼ ἐγώ νιν ἦγον ὡς γαμουμένην ,
sections.translation: For you it was I wreathed her head and led her forth as if to marriage , but now it is to slaughter I am bringing her . On you will come reproach because you did not help her ; for though not wedded to her , yet were you the loving husband of my hapless girl in name at any rate . By your beard , your right hand , and mother too I do implore you ;

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: ὄνομα γὰρ τὸ σόν μʼ ἀπώλεσʼ , ᾧ σʼ ἀμυναθεῖν χρεών —
sections.translation: for your name it was that worked my ruin , and you are bound to stand by that . Except your knees I have no altar to fly to ; and not a friend stands [*] at my side . You have heard the cruel abandoned scheme of Agamemnon ; and I , a woman , have come , as you see , to a camp of lawless sailor-folk , bold in evil ’ s cause ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: χρήσιμον δʼ , ὅταν θέλωσιν . ἢν δὲ τολμήσῃς σύ μου
sections.translation: though useful when they wish ; Now if you boldly stretch forth your arm in my behalf , our safety is assured ; but if not , we are lost .

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: δεινὸν τὸ τίκτειν καὶ φέρει φίλτρον μέγα
sections.translation: A strange thing is motherhood , carrying with it a potent spell , in which all share , so that for their children ’ s sake they will endure affliction .

sections.label: Section 919
sections.original: ὑψηλόφρων μοι θυμὸς αἴρεται πρόσω ·
sections.translation: [*] My proud spirit is stirred to range aloft , butI have learned to grieve in misfortune

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἐπίσταμαι δὲ τοῖς κακοῖσί τʼ ἀσχαλᾶν
sections.translation: and rejoice in high prosperity with equal moderation . For these are the men who can count on ordering all their life rightly by wisdom ’ s rules . True , there are cases where it is pleasant not to be too wise ,

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ἔστιν δὲ χὥπου χρήσιμον γνώμην ἔχειν .
sections.translation: but there are others , where some store of wisdom helps . Brought up in godly Chiron ’ s halls myself , I learned to keep a single heart ; and provided the Atridae lead well , I will obey them ; but when they cease from that , no more will I obey ;

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐνθάδʼ ἐν Τροίᾳ τʼ ἐλευθέραν φύσιν
sections.translation: no , but here and in Troy I will show the freedom of my nature , and , as far as in me lies , do honor to Ares with my spear . You , lady , who have suffered so cruelly from your nearest and dearest , I will , by every effort in a young man ’ s power , set right , investing you with that amount of pity

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: κοὔποτε κόρη σὴ πρὸς πατρὸς σφαγήσεται ,
sections.translation: and never shall your daughter , after being once called my bride , die by her father ’ s hand ; for I will not lend myself to your husband ’ s subtle tricks ; no ! for it will be my name that kills your child , although it does not wield the sword . Your own husband

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: πόσις σός . ἁγνὸν δʼ οὐκέτʼ ἐστὶ σῶμʼ ἐμόν ,
sections.translation: is the actual cause , but I shall no longer be guiltless , if , because of me and my marriage , this maiden perishes , she that has suffered past endurance and been the victim of affronts most strangely undeserved .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: ἐγὼ κάκιστος ἦν ἄρʼ Ἀργείων ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: So am I made the poorest wretch in Argos ;

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ἐγὼ τὸ μηδέν , Μενέλεως δʼ ἐν ἀνδράσιν ,
sections.translation: I a thing of nothing , and Menelaus counting for a man ! No son of Peleus I , but the issue of a vengeful fiend , if my name shall [*] serve your husband for the murder . No ! by Nereus , who begot my mother Thetis , in his home amid the flowing waves ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: οὐχ ἅψεται σῆς θυγατρὸς Ἀγαμέμνων ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: never shall king Agamemnon touch your daughter , no ! not even to the laying of a finger-tip upon her robe ; or Sipylus [*] , that frontier town of barbarism , the cradle of those chieftains ’ line , will be henceforth a city indeed , while Phthia ’ s name will nowhere find mention .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: πικροὺς δὲ προχύτας χέρνιβάς τʼ ἀνάξεται
sections.translation: Calchas , the seer , shall rue beginning the sacrifice with his barley-meal and lustral water . Why , what is a seer ? A man who with luck tells the truth sometimes , with frequent falsehoods , but when his luck deserts him , collapses then and there .
